topics.name: 100 - 200 Level: Legal Foundations, Constitutional Law, and Contracts
topics.description: Nigerian Legal System, Legal Methods, Constitutional Law, Law of Contract, and Customary Law.

subtopics.name: PUL 101 & PUL 102: Nigerian Legal System and Legal Methods
objectives: Trace sources of Nigerian law: English law, customary law, Islamic law, statutes, judicial precedents
Analyze hierarchy of courts in Nigeria: Supreme Court, Court of Appeal, Federal/State High Courts
Master legal reasoning, case analysis, statutory interpretation, and legal citation

subtopics.name: PUL 201 & BUL 201: Constitutional Law and Law of Contract
objectives: Analyze fundamental rights, separation of powers, judicial review, and executive emergency powers
Study elements of valid contract: offer, acceptance, consideration, intention to create legal relations, capacity
Analyze remedies for breach of contract: damages, specific performance, injunctions

topics.name: 300 Level: Criminal Law, Torts, and Commercial Law
topics.description: Criminal Law, Law of Torts, Commercial Law, and Land Law.

subtopics.name: PUL 301 & BUL 301: Criminal Law and Law of Torts
objectives: Analyze actus reus, mens rea, general defenses, murder, manslaughter, stealing, fraud, and armed robbery
Study torts of negligence, nuisance, defamation, trespass to person, and trespass to land

subtopics.name: PUL 302 & BUL 302: Land Law and Commercial Law
objectives: Analyze Land Use Act 1978, customary land tenure, Certificate of Occupancy (C of O), and mortgages
Study agency law, sale of goods, hire purchase, and negotiable instruments

topics.name: 400 - 500 Level: Equity, Evidence, Jurisprudence, Company Law, and LL.B. Thesis
topics.description: Equity & Trusts, Law of Evidence, Jurisprudence & Legal Theory, Company Law, and Thesis.

subtopics.name: PUL 401 & BUL 401: Equity & Trusts and Law of Evidence
objectives: Apply maxims of equity, equitable remedies, and express, implied, and constructive trusts
Study Evidence Act 2011: admissibility of evidence, burden of proof, hearsay, and electronic evidence

subtopics.name: PUL 501 & BUL 501: Jurisprudence, Company Law, and LL.B. Thesis
objectives: Analyze natural law, positivism, historical, sociological, and realist legal philosophies
Study Companies and Allied Matters Act (CAMA 2020): incorporation, corporate governance, shares, and winding up
Write and defend independent LL.B. legal research project thesis before Faculty Board
